title: Variables 5
description: Rust allows shadowing, where you declare a new variable with the same name. This is different from mutation.
instructions: Fix the code using shadowing to change the type of the variable.
hints: Shadowing allows you to reuse a variable name with a new `let` binding.
Unlike `mut`, shadowing lets you change the type of the variable.
Add `let` before the second assignment to shadow the variable.
explanation: Shadowing in Rust creates a new variable that happens to have the same name. It is different from `mut` because you can change the variable's type. The original variable is effectively hidden by the new one in the same or inner scope.
